requirements.summary: Add a token configuration self-check comparing configured ledger canister IDs against expected mainnet IDs and reporting mismatches without throwing.
acceptanceCriteria: A single function (or equivalent) exists that returns, per token (ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC), the configured ledger canister ID, the expected mainnet ledger canister ID, and a boolean indicating whether they match exactly.
When a token is missing a ledger canister ID, the self-check reports it as not configured and does not throw.
The expected mainnet ledger canister IDs used for the comparison are: ckBTC = mxzaz-hqaaa-aaaar-qaada-cai; ckETH = ss2fx-dyaaa-aaaar-qacoq-cai; ckUSDC = xevnm-gaaaa-aaaar-qafnq-cai.

file_operations.description: Add an expected-mainnet ledger canister ID mapping for ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC and implement a single self-check function that returns (per token) symbol, configuredLedgerCanisterId (or undefined), expectedLedgerCanisterId, and matchesExpected (false when missing/mismatched) without throwing when not configured.

requirements.summary: Add a read-only Token Ledger Diagnostics panel on the Dashboard that displays per-token configured vs expected ledger IDs and supports copy-to-clipboard JSON reporting.
acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard page shows a new diagnostics card/panel dedicated to token ledger configuration (in addition to the existing Transport Key Diagnostics).

For each of ckBTC, ckETH, ckUSDC, the panel shows: token symbol, configured ledger canister ID (or a clear “Not configured” state), and a status indicator for “Matches expected mainnet ID” vs “Mismatch”.
The panel provides a “Copy JSON” action that copies a JSON blob including timestamp and per-token fields (symbol, configuredLedgerCanisterId, expectedLedgerCanisterId, matchesExpected).
All user-facing text in this panel is in English.

file_operations.description: Create a new Dashboard diagnostics card that renders the token self-check results (ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC) and provides a “Copy JSON” action that copies a timestamped JSON diagnostic blob. Use existing shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card, Badge, Button, Separator) for consistent UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the new TokenLedgerDiagnosticsPanel into the Dashboard page so it appears as a dedicated diagnostics card/panel in addition to the existing TransportKeyDiagnosticsPanel, and ensure all displayed text is English. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a non-transfer ledger connectivity/metadata verification per configured token using read-only ICRC calls and surface success/failure and returned metadata in the diagnostics UI and JSON copy report.
acceptanceCriteria: The app performs only read-only calls for this verification (no transfer calls are made).
For each configured token, the diagnostics UI shows whether the metadata check succeeded and displays returned values (at minimum decimals) when available.

If a ledger call fails (e.g., due to network/agent/canister issues), the UI shows a non-crashing error state for that token and still allows copying diagnostics JSON including the error message/stringified failure.

file_operations.description: Add a small client helper that can perform read-only ICRC ledger metadata calls (e.g., name/symbol/decimals and/or supported standards) against a provided ledger canister ID, using the app’s existing agent/identity context where appropriate, and ensuring no transfer/write methods are invoked. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a React Query-based hook that (a) computes configured-vs-expected self-check results and (b) for each configured token performs read-only metadata calls via the ICRC client, returning per-token success, metadata (at minimum decimals when available), and a safe error string on failure (no crashes). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Extend the panel to call useTokenLedgerDiagnostics and display per-token metadata check status, show returned values (at minimum decimals) when available, and render a non-crashing error state when calls fail; ensure the “Copy JSON” report includes per-token error text (when any) alongside symbol/configured/expected/matchesExpected and metadata fields. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
